exercises.prompt: A man is found dead, alone, in a locked room. Scattered on the floor beside him are 53 playing cards — all from the same 'Bicycle' brand deck. How did he die, and why does the number 53 matter? Work out an explanation, then reveal and compare.
exercises.answer: He was a card cheat who got caught: a standard deck has 52 cards, so 53 means there was one extra card hidden in the deck — proof he'd been palming an extra card to cheat. Another player discovered the 53rd card and killed him for it.
exercises.explanation: The riddle hinges on knowing a deck has exactly 52 cards — the mismatch is the clue, not the number itself.

exercises.prompt: In 17th-century France, the gambler Chevalier de Méré believed these two bets were equally good odds. Which is actually more likely: rolling at least one 6 in four rolls of a single die, or rolling at least one double-six in twenty-four rolls of a pair of dice?
choices: They are exactly equally likely
At least one 6 in four rolls of a single die
At least one double-six in 24 rolls of two dice
Neither can be computed without more information
exercises.answer: At least one 6 in four rolls of a single die
exercises.explanation: The single-die bet succeeds about 51.8% of the time ($1-(5/6)^4$), while the double-six bet succeeds only about 49.1% of the time ($1-(35/36)^{24}$) — a gap Pascal helped explain for de Méré, one of the early sparks for probability theory.

exercises.prompt: What common English word contains three consecutive pairs of double letters (like 'oo', 'kk', 'ee' back to back)?
exercises.explanation: b-oo-kk-ee-per: the double o, double k, and double e sit back-to-back, which is unusual even among long English words.

exercises.prompt: Point Nemo, the spot in the ocean farthest from any land (over 2,600 km from the nearest shore), is often nearer to which humans than to anyone on land?
choices: Passengers on a nearby cruise ship
Astronauts aboard the International Space Station
Fishermen on remote research vessels
No humans are ever closer than the mainland
exercises.answer: Astronauts aboard the International Space Station
exercises.explanation: The ISS orbits roughly 400 km up — closer to Point Nemo than the more-than-2,600-km-away nearest land, so when it passes overhead its crew are literally the nearest people.

exercises.prompt: A man walks into a bar and asks the bartender for a glass of water. Without a word, the bartender pulls out a gun and points it at him. The man smiles, says 'thank you,' and calmly walks out. Why? Work out an explanation, then reveal and compare.
exercises.answer: The man had the hiccups and wanted a scare to cure them. The bartender realized this and pointed the gun to startle him — it worked, curing his hiccups, so he no longer needed the water and left, thankful.
exercises.explanation: The riddle hinges on treating 'water' as a symptom (hiccups) rather than thirst — the bartender solves the real problem a different way.

exercises.prompt: The total length of all the blood vessels in an adult human body, laid end to end, is roughly enough to:
choices: Drive coast-to-coast across the United States once
Circle the Earth about two and a half times
Reach the Moon
Circle the Earth about ten times
exercises.answer: Circle the Earth about two and a half times
exercises.explanation: The oft-cited estimate is about 100,000 km (60,000 miles) of vessels — Earth's circumference is about 40,000 km, so that's roughly two and a half laps; nowhere near the Moon's 384,000 km or ten full laps around Earth.

exercises.prompt: Is it possible for Player A to have a lower batting average than Player B in each of two separate baseball seasons individually, yet a higher combined average than Player B across both seasons combined?
choices: Yes — this can happen if the players had very different numbers of at-bats in each season
No — that would be a logical contradiction
Only if one player's average was negative, which is impossible
Only if they played for different teams
exercises.answer: Yes — this can happen if the players had very different numbers of at-bats in each season
exercises.explanation: This is Simpson's Paradox — it has happened in real career stats (e.g., Derek Jeter vs. David Justice, 1995-1996) because averaging averages ignores how many at-bats back each one; a season with far more at-bats pulls the combined average toward itself.

exercises.prompt: What is the only even prime number?
exercises.explanation: Every other even number is divisible by 2 and by itself, giving it a third factor — 2 is the sole exception.

exercises.prompt: Which line actually appears in the 1942 film Casablanca?
choices: "Play it again, Sam."
"Play it, Sam."
"Play it once more, Sam."
"Sam, play it for old times' sake."
exercises.explanation: The famous phrase "Play it again, Sam" never actually occurs in the film — Ilsa's real line is the shorter "Play it, Sam. Play 'As Time Goes By.'"

exercises.prompt: A drawer holds 10 pairs of socks — 20 individual socks in total, all 10 colors different. In the dark, what is the minimum number of socks you must pull out to be guaranteed a matching pair?
exercises.explanation: In the worst case you could pull one sock of each of the 10 colors (10 socks, no match yet) — the very next one, the 11th, must match one you already have.
